calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-29-2024 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-26-2023 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-27-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion And Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings; Memo Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Feb-16-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT WATERVILLE, INC. DOING BUSINESS AS ANTIOCH NISSAN, NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC. Motion And Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings

Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Scott D. Sharp, Esq. of LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, Tel: 213.250.1800, for defendants/moving party;
Timothy Lupinek, Esq. of KNIGHT LAW GROUP, LLP, Tel: 310.552.2250, for plaintiff/opposing party.

The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, DEFENDANTS WATERVILLE, INC. DOING BUSINESS AS ANTIOCH NISSAN, and NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is taken under submission by the court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Brianna Rudd, CSR#13668, Email: briannarudd@gmail.com, Tel: 209.747.1306. (302/RBU)

*********************************************************************************************

After considering oral arguments, the court issued its ruling on DEFENDANTS WATERVILLE, INC. DOING BUSINESS AS ANTIOCH NISSAN, and NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings - Granted. Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order that incorporates the court's tentative ruling. (302/RBU)

Motion And Notice Of Hearing (Motions)
Off Calendar Jan-24-2023 Continued To Feb-16-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ZACHARY POWELL, Esq. of Knight Law Group, LLP (Tel: 310-552-2250) for plaintiff;
Atty. SCOTT D. SHARP, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (Tel: 213-250-1800) for defendants Nissan North America, Inc., Waterville, Inc. dba Antioch Nissan.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS WATERVILLE, INC. DOING BUSINESS AS ANTIOCH NISSAN, and NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed parties to appear to discuss whether defendant's over-long reply brief should be considered.

Matter discussed.

The Court indicates it will consider defendant's reply brief.

The Court continues the hearing on this matter to February 16, 2023 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-29-2022 Set For Trial On Jul-31-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
